title: cerasus × subhirtella
intro: Cerasus × subhirtella, also known as the Japanese flowering cherry, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Prunus serrulata and Prunus incisa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been used to treat various health issues. The bark of Cerasus × subhirtella contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, offering health benefits such as relieving pain and reducing fever. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
